STL-SW.notes: Offer applied when certainty-seeker or DLY-OTH leads need an anchor

EX-STL.notes: High offer rate — offer functions as trust bridge not just incentive for switchers

PURCH.notes: Low offer rate — PURCH leads close on product fit and setup timeline

ABROAD.notes: High offer rate — departure date alignment creates natural urgency window

SELL.notes: SELL bridge leads need the certainty of an offer alongside the flexible exit

R2R.notes: R2R leads never receive offers — either lost on margin or the model doesn't apply

analytical-evaluator.note: Offer helps close analysis paralysis — gives them something to decide on

certainty-seeker.note: Offer acts as permanent floor on fees — reduces the perceived income risk

loss-averse-switcher.note: Offer reduces financial downside of trying a new provider

betrayal-damaged.note: Offer is the trust bridge — entry 036 key example

urgency-driven.note: Offer expiry creates dual urgency with natural time pressure

fast-path-gain-focused.note: Offer useful as differentiator vs competitors

converted_without_offer.lesson: Strong intent + right product + clear next step = conversion without incentive. Don't automatically apply offer to every lead.

timing_patterns: ABROAD leads: offer applied within 24-48h of meeting, tied to departure date
EX-STL leads: offer extended 7-14 days to allow competitor contract review
SELL bridge: offer applied in same session — they need security of the decision
June 2026 batch: all expires June 14-30 or July 1 — 7-30 day windows
DLY-FUR leads: offer expiry set beyond renovation completion estimate
Offer never applied to R2R (model mismatch or margin issue)
